frameworks.name: meta-reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: repeated reversals shift value computation from plasticity-based to dynamics-based inference

frameworks.name: synaptic-plasticity-based value learning
frameworks.one_line: incremental value updating via BLA synaptic plasticity under stable contingencies

frameworks.name: recurrent neural network model
frameworks.one_line: RNNs with continuous weight updates recapitulate plasticity-to-dynamics transition

frameworks.name: latent task structure inference
frameworks.one_line: value inferred from latent contextual structure rather than incrementally accumulated

core_question: How does the brain implement and transition between incremental and inference-based modes of value computation?
key_assumptions: value can be updated either incrementally or by inference over latent structure
basolateral amygdala participates in value computation
training regime selects which value-computation mode dominates
manipulations: stable vs. dynamically reversing odor-outcome contingencies
disruption of BLA activity before cue delivery
training RNNs with continuous weight updates
key_findings: stable training yields BLA-plasticity-dependent long-term value representations
repeated reversals enable BLA-plasticity-independent value inference with faster learning but rapid decay
BLA neurons encode value and contextual information for latent-structure-based value
pre-cue BLA disruption preferentially impairs dynamics-based value updating
interpretations: experience with volatility shifts value computation from plasticity-based to dynamics-based mode
structure-specific value inference can be learned across distinct correlation regimes
